name: Abdullah ibn Ja'far
title: Husband of Zaynab (SA)
shortDescription: Lady Zaynab's (SA) husband who stayed in Hejaz due to old age, but sent his two sons Awn and Muhammad to help the Imam.
fullStory:
Abdullah ibn Ja'far al-Tayyar was the nephew of Amir al-Mu'minin (AS) and the devoted husband of Lady Zaynab (SA). He was a very generous and respected man among the Bani Hashem.

When Imam Hussain (AS) decided to leave Mecca for Kufa, Abdullah ibn Ja'far, who could not accompany the Imam due to old age and blindness (or poor vision), wrote a letter trying to dissuade the Imam from going. He even got a letter of safe conduct for the Imam from the governor of Mecca.

But when he realized that the Imam's resolve for this journey was absolute and it was a divine duty, he sent his two young sons, 'Awn' and 'Muhammad', along with their mother Lady Zaynab (SA), and ordered them to sacrifice their lives for their uncle.

After the event of Ashura and the return of the caravan to Medina, when he was given the news of his sons' martyrdom, he proudly said: 'Praise be to God that if I could not help Hussain myself, my sons helped him.'
quoteArabic: الحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ... إِنْ لَمْ أَكُنْ واسَيْتُ حُسَيْناً بِنَفْسِي فَقَدْ واساهُ وَلَدِي...
quote: (Upon hearing of his sons' martyrdom:) Praise be to God... If I could not help Hussain with my own life, my sons helped him.
